summary: Descriptive overview of productsliability in substantive law: design defect, manufacturing defect, failure to warn.
key_points: design defect is a fundamental concept in tort law.
Legal standards for productsliability are established through case law and statutory interpretation.
Courts apply specific tests to determine productsliability in litigation.
The doctrine of productsliability has evolved through Supreme Court precedents.
Understanding productsliability requires analysis of both historical context and modern applications.
philosophy_guard_statement: This fragment describes existing legal doctrines and their applications without advocating for normative changes.
